title: English at School
description: English you can use in school life!

scenes.speech: School English works with teachers and friends! 🏫
scenes.title: English at School
scenes.content: This lesson is about English at school. Class, break time, the classroom, the library — phrases for everyday school life.

scenes.speech: Can you say subjects in English?
scenes.title: Subjects
scenes.content: math, science, English, history, music, P.E. Start from the subject you like — it's easier to remember.
scenes.highlight:
math / science / English
history / music / P.E.

scenes.speech: Magic words to ask the teacher!
scenes.title: Asking the Teacher
scenes.content: Excuse me, can I ask a question? I don't understand. The courage to ask doubles your learning speed. Raise a hand, then say it.
scenes.highlight:
Can I ask a question?
I don't understand.

scenes.title: Asking to Repeat
scenes.content: Could you say that again, please? Could you speak slowly? It's fine to miss it — just ask again.
scenes.highlight:
Could you say that again?
Could you speak slowly?

scenes.speech: Try saying classroom tools in English!
scenes.title: Classroom Tools
scenes.content: pen, pencil, eraser, notebook, textbook, ruler. Practice 'Can I borrow your pencil?' — useful in class.
scenes.highlight:
pen / pencil / eraser
notebook / textbook / ruler

scenes.title: Homework
scenes.content: What's the homework? I finished my homework. Use them with friends as-is.
scenes.highlight:
What's the homework?
I finished my homework.

scenes.speech: Talk in English during break time!
scenes.title: Break Time
scenes.content: Let's play together! Want to join us? Inviting friends in English builds an English-speaking gang.
scenes.highlight:
Let's play together!
Want to join us?

scenes.title: Tests
scenes.content: The test was hard. I did my best. After a test, sharing 'I did my best' is a universal encouragement.
scenes.highlight:
The test was hard.
I did my best.

scenes.title: Going Home
scenes.content: See you tomorrow! Good job today! End-of-day greetings convey care for the friends you'll see again.
scenes.highlight:
See you tomorrow!
Good job today!

scenes.speech: Quiz! How do you politely ask 'Could you say it again?'
scenes.quizQuestion: Which is the politest way to ask for repetition?
quizChoices: Could you say that again, please?
scenes.quizExplanation: Could you say that again, please? is the politest. The 'please' is key.
